oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for AF with rapid ventricular rate in WPW is procainamide (NOT digoxin/verapamil — they accelerate accessory pathway)

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for hypertensive emergency in aortic dissection is IV labetalol or esmolol (beta-blocker first, then vasodilator)

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Prinzmetal angina: spasm of coronary artery; DOC is calcium channel blocker (verapamil/diltiazem); nitrates also used

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Vaughan Williams Class Ia antiarrhythmics: quinidine, procainamide, disopyramide — Na+ block + prolong refractory period

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Dressler syndrome (post-MI pericarditis) occurs 2-10 weeks after MI; treat with aspirin/NSAIDs; steroids if refractory

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: S4 gallop indicates decreased LV compliance (hypertensive heart disease, hypertrophic CM); S3 indicates volume overload

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Normal QTc: <440 ms men, <460 ms women; prolonged QTc leads to risk of torsades de pointes

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Cardiac tamponade triad (Beck's): hypotension + raised JVP + muffled heart sounds
oneliners.mnemonic: Beck's 3 B's: BP down, Big JVP, Bad sounds

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Eisenmenger syndrome: L to R shunt reversed to R to L due to pulmonary HTN; cyanosis appears late; lung transplant only cure

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for acute STEMI is primary PCI within 90 min (door-to-balloon); thrombolysis if PCI unavailable within 120 min

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Hypertrophic obstructive CM: systolic murmur increases with Valsalva and standing (decreased preload); decreases with squatting

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Atrial myxoma: commonest primary cardiac tumor; 75% in LA; causes ball-valve obstruction + tumor plop on auscultation

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for minimal change disease in children is prednisolone; 80% respond within 8 weeks

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Fractional excretion of Na (FeNa) <1% indicates prerenal AKI; >2% indicates intrinsic renal AKI (ATN)

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Nephrotic range proteinuria: >3.5 g/day in adults, >40 mg/m2/hr in children

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Alport syndrome: X-linked mutation in type IV collagen (COL4A5); sensorineural deafness + hematuria + renal failure

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Renal tubular acidosis type 1 (distal): inability to acidify urine below pH 5.5; causes hypokalemia + nephrocalcinosis

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: ADPKD: mutation in PKD1 (chr 16) or PKD2 (chr 4); associated with berry aneurysms, liver cysts, mitral valve prolapse

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Goodpasture syndrome: anti-GBM antibody against alpha-3 chain of type IV collagen; lung hemorrhage + rapidly progressive GN

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for lupus nephritis class III/IV is mycophenolate mofetil (induction) + steroids; cyclophosphamide alternative

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for thyroid storm is PTU (blocks synthesis + peripheral T4 to T3 conversion); propranolol controls symptoms
oneliners.mnemonic: PTU in storm: Peripheral conversion Too blocked

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: MEN 1 (Wermer): Parathyroid + Pituitary + Pancreas (3 P's); gene MENIN on chromosome 11

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: MEN 2A (Sipple): Medullary thyroid Ca + Pheochromocytoma + Parathyroid; RET proto-oncogene mutation

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Conn syndrome (primary hyperaldosteronism): hypertension + hypokalemia + metabolic alkalosis; low renin

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for Graves' disease in pregnancy (1st trimester) is PTU; switch to carbimazole after 1st trimester

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Acromegaly: IGF-1 best screening test; GH suppression test with glucose is confirmatory; transphenoidal surgery is DOC

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Adrenal crisis: DOC is IV hydrocortisone 100 mg stat; then normal saline; fludrocortisone added when oral tolerated

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Central DI responds to desmopressin; nephrogenic DI does NOT respond; water deprivation test differentiates

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Whipple triad for insulinoma: symptoms with fasting, blood glucose <45 mg/dL, relief with glucose administration

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Hypothyroidism commonest cause worldwide is iodine deficiency; in developed countries it is Hashimoto thyroiditis

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for status epilepticus is IV lorazepam (0.1 mg/kg); if unavailable give diazepam; then phenytoin/levetiracetam

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Wilson disease: copper deposition; low ceruloplasmin; Kayser-Fleischer rings; DOC is D-penicillamine; zinc for maintenance

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Myasthenia gravis: anti-AChR antibody; tensilon (edrophonium) test positive; DOC pyridostigmine + immunosuppression

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Guillain-Barre syndrome: ascending flaccid paralysis + areflexia after infection (Campylobacter); CSF shows albuminocytological dissociation

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Wernicke encephalopathy (thiamine def): confusion + ataxia + ophthalmoplegia triad; give thiamine BEFORE glucose

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Horner syndrome: ptosis + miosis + anhidrosis (sympathetic chain lesion); commonest cause is Pancoast tumor

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for trigeminal neuralgia is carbamazepine; if refractory, oxcarbazepine or surgical microvascular decompression

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Normal pressure hydrocephalus: wet (incontinence) + wobbly (ataxia) + wacky (dementia) triad; responds to LP drainage
oneliners.mnemonic: 3 W's of NPH: Wet, Wobbly, Wacky

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for acute gout attack is NSAIDs (indomethacin); colchicine if NSAIDs contraindicated; steroids if both CI

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Allopurinol: xanthine oxidase inhibitor reduces uric acid synthesis; do NOT start during acute attack (may prolong it)

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: SLE: anti-dsDNA (most specific) + anti-Smith (most specific); ANA (most sensitive screening test)

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Rheumatoid arthritis: anti-CCP (most specific); RF positive in 70-80%; symmetric small joint arthritis + morning stiffness >1 hr

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Ankylosing spondylitis: HLA-B27 associated; bamboo spine on X-ray; DOC NSAIDs; TNF-inhibitors if refractory

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Felty syndrome: RA + splenomegaly + neutropenia (triad); increased risk of lymphoma

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: H. pylori eradication: triple therapy (PPI + clarithromycin + amoxicillin) for 14 days; test-and-treat strategy

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Child-Pugh score parameters: Bilirubin, Albumin, PT, Ascites, Encephalopathy (BAPAE); grades A/B/C

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Achalasia cardia: failure of LES relaxation due to loss of Auerbach plexus; barium shows rat-tail/bird-beak appearance

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: DOC for C. difficile colitis (mild-moderate) is oral vancomycin or fidaxomicin (NOT metronidazole per 2021 guidelines)

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Primary biliary cholangitis: anti-mitochondrial antibody (AMA-M2); cholestatic picture; DOC ursodeoxycholic acid

oneliners.subject: Medicine
oneliners.fact: Budd-Chiari syndrome: hepatic vein thrombosis; triad of abdominal pain + hepatomegaly + ascites; caudate lobe hypertrophies

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Courvoisier's law: palpable, non-tender gallbladder in jaundiced patient indicates malignant obstruction (NOT gallstones)

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: DOC for appendicitis is appendicectomy (laparoscopic preferred); antibiotics alone acceptable in uncomplicated cases

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Ramstedt pyloromyotomy is DOC for hypertrophic pyloric stenosis; correct hypochloremic hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis first

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Ranson's criteria: 3 or more at admission (or 48 hr) indicates severe pancreatitis; APACHE II >=8 also indicates severe

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Most common site of GI bleeding in adults: duodenal ulcer (anterior wall perforates, posterior wall bleeds)

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Richter's hernia: only part of bowel wall (antimesenteric border) in hernia sac; may strangulate without obstruction

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Spigelian hernia: through linea semilunaris lateral to rectus; interparietal (between muscle layers); difficult to diagnose

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Mirizzi syndrome: external compression of CBD by stone in Hartmann's pouch of GB; causes obstructive jaundice

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: DOC for esophageal varices: acute bleed = terlipressin + endoscopic band ligation; prophylaxis = propranolol

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Pseudomyxoma peritonei: mucinous ascites from appendiceal mucocele; jelly belly; treated by peritonectomy + HIPEC

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Compartment syndrome: 6 P's = Pain, Pressure, Paralysis, Paresthesia, Pallor, Pulselessness; DOC is fasciotomy

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Colles fracture: distal radius fracture with dorsal angulation (dinner fork deformity); FOOSH injury mechanism

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Avascular necrosis of femoral head: commonest cause is trauma (femoral neck fracture); also steroids, sickle cell, alcohol
oneliners.mnemonic: TRASH: Trauma, RA, Alcohol, Steroids, Hemoglobinopathy

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Scaphoid fracture: FOOSH injury; tenderness in anatomical snuffbox; X-ray may be normal initially; MRI is most sensitive

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: DOC for septic arthritis is IV cloxacillin (Staph aureus most common); joint washout + drainage

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Osgood-Schlatter disease: apophysitis of tibial tubercle in adolescents; traction injury; self-limiting

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Erb's palsy (C5-C6): waiter's tip position; loss of abduction, lateral rotation, supination; affects deltoid, supraspinatus
oneliners.mnemonic: Waiter's tip = Upper trunk C5-C6 injury

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Volkmann's ischemic contracture: compartment syndrome of forearm leading to fibrosis; flexion deformity of wrist and fingers

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Commonest malignancy of kidney in adults: clear cell RCC (from PCT cells); associated with VHL gene mutation on chr 3p

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Wilms tumor (nephroblastoma): commonest renal tumor in children; WT1 gene; triad = mass + hematuria + hypertension

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: DOC for uncomplicated UTI in non-pregnant females is oral nitrofurantoin or trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole for 3-5 days

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: BPH: commonest site is transitional zone; PSA elevated but not specific; DOC alpha-blocker (tamsulosin) +/- 5-ARI

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Cryptorchidism: undescended testis; increases risk of malignancy even after orchiopexy; germ cell tumors commonest

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Fournier's gangrene: necrotizing fasciitis of perineum/scrotum; polymicrobial; DOC = emergency debridement + broad-spectrum ABx

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Aortic aneurysm >5.5 cm in men (>5 cm women) warrants elective repair; ruptured AAA is surgical emergency

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Leriche syndrome: aortoiliac occlusion triad = bilateral claudication + impotence + absent femoral pulses

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: DOC for DVT is LMWH initially then warfarin (INR 2-3) for 3-6 months; DOACs (rivaroxaban) now preferred

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Varicose veins: incompetence of sapheno-femoral junction (SFJ) commonest; Trendelenburg test used for assessment

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Carotid artery stenosis >70% with symptoms warrants carotid endarterectomy; asymptomatic >60% is controversial

oneliners.subject: Surgery
oneliners.fact: Buerger's disease (thromboangiitis obliterans): young male smoker + rest pain + Raynaud's; DOC = smoking cessation

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Iron deficiency anemia: microcytic hypochromic; serum ferritin LOW (best indicator of iron stores); TIBC HIGH

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Sickle cell anemia: HbS (beta6 Glu to Val mutation); sickle in hypoxia; DOC for crisis = IV fluids + analgesia + O2
oneliners.mnemonic: Glu to Val = SiCkle cell switch

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: G6PD deficiency: X-linked; Heinz bodies + bite cells on smear; hemolysis triggered by oxidative stress (primaquine, dapsone)

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Beta-thalassemia major: HbF predominant; Erlenmeyer flask deformity; crew-cut skull X-ray; needs regular transfusion

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH): GPI anchor deficiency (PIGA mutation); complement-mediated hemolysis; DOC eculizumab

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Philadelphia chromosome: t(9;22) BCR-ABL; seen in CML (100%) + ALL (25%); treated with imatinib (TKI)
oneliners.mnemonic: Ph chromosome = Philadelphia cream cheese t(9;22)

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Reed-Sternberg cells (owl-eye nucleoli): pathognomonic of Hodgkin lymphoma; CD15+, CD30+
oneliners.mnemonic: RS cells = Two owl eyes watching

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Hemophilia A: factor VIII deficiency; X-linked recessive; PTT prolonged, PT normal; DOC recombinant FVIII

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: DIC: consumptive coagulopathy; low fibrinogen + elevated D-dimer + low platelets + prolonged PT/PTT; treat underlying cause

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Polycythemia vera: JAK2 V617F mutation (95%); low EPO; treated with phlebotomy +/- hydroxyurea; risk of Budd-Chiari

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Aplastic anemia: pancytopenia with hypocellular marrow; commonest cause = idiopathic (autoimmune); DOC allogeneic HSCT

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Von Willebrand disease: commonest inherited bleeding disorder; Type 1 (partial VWF deficiency); DOC desmopressin (DDAVP) for Type 1
oneliners.mnemonic: VWD = Very Widespread Disease (commonest hereditary bleeding)

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Commonest cancer worldwide in males: lung cancer (incidence); prostate cancer (USA); oral cancer (India)
oneliners.mnemonic: India = Oral, World = Lung cancer dominates

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Tumor lysis syndrome: hyperkalemia + hyperphosphatemia + hypocalcemia + hyperuricemia after chemotherapy; DOC rasburicase
oneliners.mnemonic: KPCU: K up, P up, Ca down, Uric acid up

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: p53 tumor suppressor (chr 17p): guardian of genome; mutated in 50%+ of all cancers; Li-Fraumeni syndrome

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Paraneoplastic syndromes: SIADH (small cell lung Ca), PTHrP hypercalcemia (squamous cell Ca), Eaton-Lambert (small cell Ca)

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Krukenberg tumor: gastric Ca metastasis to bilateral ovaries via transcoelomic spread; signet ring cells

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Multiple myeloma: M-spike on SPEP; Bence-Jones proteins in urine; lytic bone lesions (punched out); CRAB criteria
oneliners.mnemonic: CRAB: Calcium up, Renal failure, Anemia, Bone lesions

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Burkitt lymphoma: t(8;14) MYC-IgH translocation; starry sky pattern; EBV associated; fastest growing human tumor

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: PSA >10 ng/mL highly suggestive of prostate cancer; Gleason score grades aggressiveness; DOC localized = radical prostatectomy

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: CA-125 elevated in ovarian cancer; NOT specific (also elevated in endometriosis, PID); used for monitoring, not diagnosis

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Mesothelioma: associated with asbestos exposure; calretinin positive; poor prognosis; commonest in pleura

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: IgA nephropathy (Berger's disease): commonest GN worldwide; hematuria 1-3 days after URTI (synpharyngitic)

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS): commonest cause of nephrotic syndrome in adults (esp. Black patients); poor prognosis

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Membranous nephropathy: commonest cause of nephrotic syndrome in white adults; anti-PLA2R antibody; spike and dome pattern
oneliners.mnemonic: Spike and dome = Membranous nephropathy EM

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Diabetic nephropathy: Kimmelstiel-Wilson lesion (nodular glomerulosclerosis); GBM thickening; microalbuminuria is earliest feature

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: RPGN (rapidly progressive GN): crescent formation on biopsy; type II = immune complex; type I = anti-GBM; type III = pauci-immune

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Amyloidosis kidney: Congo red stain shows apple-green birefringence; AA amyloid in chronic inflammation; AL in myeloma

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Renal cell carcinoma: clear cell type commonest; 3p deletion (VHL); triad = hematuria + loin pain + mass (present in only 10%)
oneliners.mnemonic: RCC classic triad is rare to see all 3 together

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Minimal change disease: electron microscopy shows podocyte foot process effacement; NO immune deposits on IF; responds to steroids

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Caseating granuloma: TB (commonest); sarcoidosis has NON-caseating granulomas; distinction is key exam point

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Psammoma bodies: papillary thyroid Ca, serous cystadenocarcinoma ovary, meningioma, mesothelioma (PSaMMoMa)

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Councilman bodies (acidophilic apoptotic hepatocytes): seen in yellow fever and viral hepatitis

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Mallory-Denk bodies (cytokeratin aggregates in hepatocytes): seen in alcoholic hepatitis, NASH, Wilson disease

oneliners.subject: Pathology
oneliners.fact: Aschoff nodules: pathognomonic of rheumatic fever; perivascular granuloma with Anitschkow cells (caterpillar cells)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Atropine mechanism: competitive muscarinic antagonist; increases HR (blocks vagal tone); causes mydriasis + cycloplegia

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Prazosin: selective alpha-1 blocker; used in BPH + hypertension; first-dose hypotension is major side effect

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Propranolol: non-selective beta-blocker; contraindicated in asthma, Prinzmetal angina, COPD; used in thyroid storm

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Neostigmine: reversible AChE inhibitor; does NOT cross BBB (charged); used to reverse NMJ blockade; antidote = atropine

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Organophosphate poisoning: excess ACh; SLUDGE (Salivation, Lacrimation, Urination, Defecation, GI cramps, Emesis); DOC atropine + pralidoxime

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Physostigmine: reversible AChE inhibitor; CROSSES BBB (uncharged); antidote for anticholinergic toxidrome
oneliners.mnemonic: Physo crosses BBB = enters Physiology of brain

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Clonidine: central alpha-2 agonist; reduces sympathetic outflow; used in HTN + opioid withdrawal + ADHD; rebound HTN on sudden withdrawal

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Botulinum toxin: blocks ACh release at NMJ by cleaving SNARE proteins; used in dystonia, spasticity, cosmetic use

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Metoprolol: cardioselective beta-1 blocker (at low dose); safe in mild asthma; used in post-MI, heart failure, hypertension
oneliners.mnemonic: Metro beta-1 = Metro only for heart (selective)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Pilocarpine: direct muscarinic agonist; used in glaucoma (reduces IOP by increasing aqueous outflow via trabecular meshwork)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Digoxin toxicity: visual disturbances (yellow-green halos), nausea, arrhythmias; hypokalemia potentiates toxicity

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Hydralazine: direct arteriolar vasodilator; causes reflex tachycardia; DOC for HTN in pregnancy (with methyldopa, labetalol)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Nitrates mechanism: donate NO, activate guanylyl cyclase, increase cGMP, smooth muscle relaxation, venodilation (preload decreases)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Amiodarone: class III antiarrhythmic; side effects: pulmonary fibrosis, thyroid dysfunction, corneal deposits, photosensitivity
oneliners.mnemonic: Amio = All organs hit by side effects

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Statins: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors; side effect = myopathy (monitor CK); rhabdomyolysis rare but serious

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: ACE inhibitors: contraindicated in bilateral renal artery stenosis + pregnancy (teratogenic); cause dry cough (bradykinin accumulation)
oneliners.mnemonic: ACE = Avoid Cough (bradykinin), avoid in bilateral RAS

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Sacubitril-valsartan (ARNi): neprilysin inhibitor + ARB; used in HFrEF; do NOT combine with ACE inhibitor (angioedema risk)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Furosemide: loop diuretic; inhibits Na-K-2Cl cotransporter in thick ascending limb; causes hypokalemia + ototoxicity
oneliners.mnemonic: FURO = Flush out K and hearing (ototoxicity)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Penicillin mechanism: inhibits transpeptidase (PBP) preventing peptidoglycan cross-linking, leading to cell wall lysis

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Chloramphenicol side effects: grey baby syndrome (neonates), aplastic anemia (idiosyncratic), reversible bone marrow suppression

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Aminoglycosides: concentration-dependent killing; nephrotoxicity + ototoxicity (vestibular + cochlear); synergistic with beta-lactams

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Fluoroquinolones: inhibit DNA gyrase (topoisomerase II in bacteria); tendinopathy + QT prolongation; avoid in children

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Tetracycline: chelates with divalent cations (Ca, Mg, Fe); take on empty stomach; teeth discoloration in children
oneliners.mnemonic: Tetra Tricks: avoid dairy, not for kids

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Metronidazole: DOC for anaerobes, Giardia, Trichomonas, amoeba; disulfiram-like reaction with alcohol

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: DOC for MRSA is vancomycin (IV); for skin/soft tissue MRSA = linezolid or daptomycin; teicoplanin is alternative

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Rifampicin: hepatic enzyme inducer (CYP450); turns body fluids orange-red; used in TB + leprosy + meningococcal prophylaxis

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Azithromycin: azalide (macrolide derivative); long half-life (68 hrs); single-dose for chlamydia; inhibits 50S ribosomal subunit
oneliners.mnemonic: Azithro = 5 days course but works 10 days

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: DOC for Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PCP) is TMP-SMX (co-trimoxazole); prophylaxis when CD4 <200 in HIV
oneliners.mnemonic: TMP-SMX = The Main Protection for PCP

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Linezolid: oxazolidinone; inhibits 70S initiation complex; causes thrombocytopenia + serotonin syndrome with SSRIs

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Cephalosporins 3rd generation (ceftriaxone): best CSF penetration; DOC for bacterial meningitis in adults + empirical sepsis

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Phenytoin: Na+ channel blocker; zero-order kinetics (saturable); side effects = gingival hyperplasia, hirsutism, megaloblastic anemia
oneliners.mnemonic: Phenytoin = gums grow, hair grows, blood fails

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Valproate: broad-spectrum AED; Na+ channel block + GABA enhancement; teratogenic (neural tube defects); give folic acid
oneliners.mnemonic: Val + Folic acid mandatory in women of childbearing age

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Clozapine: atypical antipsychotic; most effective for treatment-resistant schizophrenia; causes agranulocytosis (monitor CBC weekly)
oneliners.mnemonic: Clozapine Clears schizophrenia but Clogs WBCs

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Lithium toxicity: tremor, polyuria, confusion, seizures; narrow therapeutic index (0.6-1.2 mEq/L); nephrotoxic long-term

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: SSRIs: DOC for depression + OCD + panic disorder; serotonin syndrome risk with MAOIs; sexual dysfunction common side effect

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Haloperidol: typical antipsychotic (D2 blocker); causes EPS (akathisia, dystonia, parkinsonism, tardive dyskinesia)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Benzodiazepines: positive allosteric modulator of GABA-A; increase frequency of Cl- channel opening; antidote = flumazenil

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Levodopa + carbidopa: dopamine precursor for Parkinson's; carbidopa prevents peripheral conversion so more L-dopa enters brain
oneliners.mnemonic: Carbidopa = Chaperone escorting L-dopa to brain

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Methotrexate: DHFR inhibitor reducing THF, decreasing purines + DNA synthesis; used in RA, psoriasis, ectopic; antidote = leucovorin
oneliners.mnemonic: MTX = Must Take eXtra folinic acid (leucovorin)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Warfarin: inhibits vitamin K epoxide reductase (VKORC1); affects factors II, VII, IX, X, Protein C, S; antidote = vitamin K + FFP
oneliners.mnemonic: Warfarin Wrecks Vit K factors (1972 = affected)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Heparin: activates antithrombin III to inactivate thrombin (IIa) and Xa; antidote = protamine sulfate; HIT = type II (IgG)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: NSAIDs mechanism: inhibit COX-1 + COX-2 reducing prostaglandin synthesis; aspirin irreversibly inhibits (acetylates) COX

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Cisplatin side effects: nephrotoxicity + ototoxicity + peripheral neuropathy; dose-limiting = nephrotoxicity; prehydration essential
oneliners.mnemonic: Cisplatin = Cis-Platinum hurts kidneys and ears

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: Tamoxifen: selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM); used in ER+ breast cancer; risk of endometrial carcinoma + DVT
oneliners.mnemonic: Tamoxifen = Treats breast but Torments uterus (endometrial Ca)

oneliners.subject: Pharmacology
oneliners.fact: DOC for malaria (chloroquine-resistant P. falciparum) is artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT); artemether-lumefantrine preferred
oneliners.mnemonic: ACT fast against resistant falciparum malaria

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: DOC for preeclampsia (antihypertensive) is labetalol IV or hydralazine IV; oral nifedipine also used; NOT ACE inhibitors in pregnancy

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Magnesium sulfate: DOC for eclampsia seizure prophylaxis and treatment; antidote = calcium gluconate
oneliners.mnemonic: Mag sulfate = Mighty anticonvulsant in eclampsia

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Placenta previa: painless bright red vaginal bleeding in 3rd trimester; NO vaginal examination; diagnosis by USG

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Abruptio placentae: painful dark red bleeding; concealed/revealed; associated with hypertension + trauma; DIC risk

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Bishop score >=6 indicates favorable cervix for induction; >=8 means induction as likely to succeed as spontaneous labor
oneliners.mnemonic: Bishop 8 = Baby coming easily; 6 = induction ok

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Gestational diabetes: screened at 24-28 weeks with 50g GCT; diagnosed with 75g OGTT; DOC metformin or insulin

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Rh incompatibility: Rh-negative mother with Rh-positive fetus; anti-D given at 28 weeks + within 72 hrs of delivery

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Oxytocin: released from posterior pituitary (made in hypothalamus); stimulates uterine contractions; used for induction of labor

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Postpartum hemorrhage (PPH): blood loss >500 mL vaginal, >1000 mL CS; commonest cause = uterine atony (4 T's)

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: DOC for PPH due to atony: oxytocin IV first; then ergometrine; then carboprost (PGF2-alpha); then B-Lynch suture/hysterectomy

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Ectopic pregnancy: commonest site = ampulla of fallopian tube (70%); risk factors = PID, previous ectopic, IUD

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: HELLP syndrome: Hemolysis + Elevated Liver enzymes + Low Platelets; variant of severe preeclampsia; DOC delivery

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Hydatidiform mole: complete mole (46XX, all paternal) leads to snowstorm USG, no fetal parts; risk of choriocarcinoma 2-3%

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: DOC for hyperemesis gravidarum: IV fluids + thiamine (prevent Wernicke) + ondansetron/metoclopramide antiemetics

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Preterm labor (<37 weeks): DOC tocolysis = nifedipine (or terbutaline); betamethasone for fetal lung maturity at 24-34 weeks
oneliners.mnemonic: Nifedipine = Natural tocolytic choice in preterm

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: PROM (prelabor rupture of membranes): >37 weeks = induce labor; <37 weeks = conservative management with antibiotics

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Cord prolapse: emergency C-section; meanwhile knee-chest position + manually elevate presenting part off cord

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: GDM offspring risks: macrosomia, hypoglycemia, polycythemia, respiratory distress, hyperbilirubinemia

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Amniocentesis: performed at 15-18 weeks; karyotyping, fetal lung maturity (L:S ratio >2 = mature), AFP for NTD detection

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Fetal distress: late decelerations most ominous (uteroplacental insufficiency); variable = cord compression; early = head compression
oneliners.mnemonic: VEAL CHOP: Variable=Cord, Early=Head, Accel=ok, Late=Placenta

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS): LH:FSH ratio >2:1; hyperandrogenism + oligomenorrhea + polycystic ovaries (Rotterdam criteria)

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: DOC for PCOS (ovulation induction) is clomiphene citrate; metformin for insulin resistance; OCP for menstrual regulation

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Endometriosis: ectopic endometrium; chocolate cysts (endometrioma); CA-125 elevated; DOC GnRH agonists or surgery

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Cervical cancer: HPV 16 (squamous, most common) and HPV 18 (adenocarcinoma); PAP smear screening; DOC chemoradiation

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Endometrial cancer: commonest gynecological cancer; postmenopausal bleeding = endometrial cancer until proven otherwise

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Ovarian cancer: commonest = serous cystadenocarcinoma; presents late (Stage III/IV); CA-125 marker; BRCA1/2 mutations

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Fibroid uterus: commonest benign gynecological tumor; intramural commonest type; heavy menstrual bleeding; DOC GnRH agonists or myomectomy

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Emergency contraception: levonorgestrel (within 72 hrs); ulipristal acetate (within 120 hrs); copper IUD (within 5 days, most effective)
oneliners.mnemonic: Cu IUD = most effective emergency contraception

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Asherman syndrome: intrauterine adhesions post D&C; secondary amenorrhea + infertility; hysteroscopic adhesiolysis DOC

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Turner syndrome (45,XO): streak ovaries + primary amenorrhea + short stature + webbed neck + coarctation of aorta
oneliners.mnemonic: Turner = Too few (45), Too short, Too webby neck

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Bartholin gland: located at 5 and 7 o'clock positions in vestibule; bartholinitis/abscess DOC = incision and marsupialization

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Stress urinary incontinence: commonest in multiparous women; DOC pelvic floor exercises (Kegel); surgical = mid-urethral sling

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Meigs syndrome: ovarian fibroma + ascites + right pleural effusion (triad); CA-125 may be elevated but benign
oneliners.mnemonic: Meigs = Mystery benign fibroma with effusion

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Vesicovaginal fistula: commonest cause in developing world = obstructed labor; in developed world = gynecological surgery
oneliners.mnemonic: VVF in India = Obstructed labor complication

oneliners.subject: OBG
oneliners.fact: Oral contraceptive pill: estrogen + progestin; contraindicated in smokers >35 years + migraine with aura + DVT history
oneliners.mnemonic: OCP = Avoid if old smoker (>35) or aura migraine

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Kwashiorkor: protein deficiency with adequate calories; edema + skin changes (flaky paint) + fatty liver; serum albumin <2.8 g/dL

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Marasmus: severe protein-calorie malnutrition; baggy pants appearance; no edema; skin and bones; ribs prominent
oneliners.mnemonic: Marasmus = Matched stick arms and legs

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Vitamin D deficiency (rickets): craniotabes (earliest sign), rachitic rosary, Harrison's sulcus; DOC vitamin D + calcium
oneliners.mnemonic: Rachitic rosary = Rickets beads on ribs

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Vitamin A deficiency: Bitot's spots (earliest reversible sign) + night blindness; xerophthalmia; DOC high-dose vitamin A
oneliners.mnemonic: Bitot = Bright spots of Vit A deficiency

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Vitamin K deficiency: hemorrhagic disease of newborn; prophylactic vitamin K 1 mg IM at birth prevents bleeding
oneliners.mnemonic: VitK at birth = Vital for neonatal clotting

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Breast milk is best for infants; WHO recommends exclusive breastfeeding for 6 months; contraindicated in HIV+ mothers (in India)

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Zinc deficiency: acrodermatitis enteropathica (perioral + perianal rash) + alopecia + diarrhea; DOC oral zinc supplementation
oneliners.mnemonic: Zinc deficiency = Zonal skin rash + alopecia

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Iron deficiency in children: most common nutritional deficiency worldwide; peaks at 6 months-3 years; DOC = oral ferrous sulfate
oneliners.mnemonic: Fe deficiency = No. 1 nutritional problem globally

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Kawasaki disease: fever >5 days + 4/5 features (rash, conjunctivitis, lymph node, oral changes, extremity changes); DOC IVIG + aspirin

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Measles (rubeola): prodrome CCCK (cough, coryza, conjunctivitis, Koplik spots); maculopapular rash cephalocaudal spread
oneliners.mnemonic: Koplik spots = Pathognomonic of measles (before rash)

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Mumps: parotitis + orchitis (post-pubertal males); pancreatitis; encephalitis; elevated serum amylase; prevented by MMR vaccine

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Whooping cough (Bordetella pertussis): inspiratory whoop + post-tussive vomiting; lymphocytosis; DOC azithromycin

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Epiglottitis: H. influenzae type b; tripod position + drooling + muffled voice; cherry-red epiglottis; DOC airway + IV cefotaxime

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Croup (laryngotracheobronchitis): parainfluenza virus; steeple sign on X-ray; barking cough; DOC nebulized epinephrine + steroids

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Dengue hemorrhagic fever: thrombocytopenia + leukopenia + rising hematocrit (plasma leak); NS1 antigen earliest marker; DOC supportive

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Typhoid fever: rose spots + relative bradycardia + splenomegaly; Widal test (unreliable); DOC ceftriaxone or azithromycin
oneliners.mnemonic: Rose spots + slow pulse = Typhoid clue

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Meningococcal meningitis: petechial/purpuric rash + meningism; Waterhouse-Friderichsen syndrome (bilateral adrenal hemorrhage); DOC IV benzylpenicillin

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Neonatal sepsis: GBS + E. coli commonest in early onset (<72 hrs); DOC ampicillin + gentamicin; late onset = Staph epidermidis

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: APGAR score: Appearance, Pulse, Grimace, Activity, Respiration; scored at 1 and 5 minutes; 7 or above = normal
oneliners.mnemonic: APGAR = A Pretty Good Assessment Really

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Transient tachypnea of newborn (TTN): wet lung; residual fetal lung fluid; CS babies at risk; resolves in 24-48 hrs; O2 support

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: RDS (hyaline membrane disease): surfactant deficiency; premature infants; ground glass + air bronchogram on CXR; DOC surfactant therapy

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Physiological jaundice: appears day 2-3, peaks day 4-5, resolves by day 7-10; pathological if appears within 24 hrs

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Kernicterus (bilirubin encephalopathy): bilirubin deposits in basal ganglia; DOC phototherapy; exchange transfusion if severe

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Neonatal hypoglycemia: blood glucose <45 mg/dL in term neonate; commonest cause = IDM (infant of diabetic mother); DOC IV dextrose
oneliners.mnemonic: IDM baby = hypoglycemia risk at birth

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Patent ductus arteriosus: prostaglandin maintains patency; DOC for closure = indomethacin or ibuprofen (premature); surgical ligation if failed
oneliners.mnemonic: PDA loves PGs; Indomethacin (anti-PG) closes it

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Tracheoesophageal fistula: commonest type = EA with distal TEF (85%); polyhydramnios + inability to pass NG tube to stomach

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Down syndrome (trisomy 21): Brushfield spots, simian crease, sandal gap toe; associated with ALL, Hirschsprung, Duodenal atresia

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Febrile seizures: age 6 months-5 years; simple (<15 min, generalized, once in 24 hr); DOC diazepam PR for prolonged seizure
oneliners.mnemonic: Simple febrile = good prognosis, no AED needed

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Reye syndrome: aspirin in viral illness leads to hepatic encephalopathy + microvesicular steatosis; AVOID aspirin in children <12
oneliners.mnemonic: Aspirin + Virus = Reye's risk in children

oneliners.subject: Paediatrics
oneliners.fact: Intussusception: 3 months-2 years; currant jelly stools + intermittent colicky pain; DOC pneumatic/hydrostatic reduction

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Sensitivity = TP/(TP+FN); ability to detect true positives; high sensitivity = good screening test (rules OUT disease if negative)
oneliners.mnemonic: SnNOut: Sensitive test Negative = rule Out

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Specificity = TN/(TN+FP); ability to correctly identify negatives; high specificity = good confirmatory test (rules IN if positive)
oneliners.mnemonic: SpPIn: Specific test Positive = rule In

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Positive predictive value (PPV) depends on prevalence; increases as prevalence increases; NOT a fixed characteristic of the test

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Relative Risk (RR): ratio of incidence in exposed vs unexposed; used in cohort studies; RR=1 means no association

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Odds Ratio (OR): used in case-control studies; approximates RR when disease is rare (prevalence <10%)
oneliners.mnemonic: OR in cAse-cOntrol = backwards study design

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Herd immunity threshold = 1 - (1/R0); for measles R0=12-18, threshold ~92-95%; for polio ~83%

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Case fatality rate (CFR) = deaths from disease / total cases x 100; NOT same as mortality rate (which uses population denominator)
oneliners.mnemonic: CFR = cases who died / total cases (not population)

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Epidemic curve: common source = sharp peak; propagated = multiple peaks with serial interval; point source = bell-shaped
oneliners.mnemonic: Sharp peak = Point source epidemic curve shape

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Type I error (alpha): rejecting true null hypothesis (false positive); p-value is probability of type I error; conventional alpha = 0.05

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Type II error (beta): accepting false null hypothesis (false negative); power = 1-beta; increasing sample size reduces beta
oneliners.mnemonic: beta = wrongly REJECT alternative (miss true disease)

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Normal distribution: mean = median = mode; 68-95-99.7 rule (1SD-2SD-3SD); basis for parametric statistical tests

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Chi-square test: used for categorical data comparison; requires expected frequency >5 in each cell; Fisher's exact if not met

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Number needed to treat (NNT) = 1/ARR (absolute risk reduction); lower NNT = more effective treatment

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: National Tuberculosis Elimination Programme (NTEP): target TB elimination by 2025 (25 years ahead of global SDG); uses DOTS strategy
oneliners.mnemonic: NTEP 2025 = India's ambitious TB elimination target

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Universal Immunization Programme (UIP): BCG at birth; Hep B at 0-6-10-14 weeks; IPV + fOPV; MR vaccine at 9 months + 16-24 months

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Pulse polio immunization: OPV given on national immunization days; India declared polio-free in 2014

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: NTEP DOTS regimen: 2-month intensive phase (HRZE) + 4-month continuation phase (HR); treatment under DOTS supervisor

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: NHM (National Health Mission): includes NRHM (rural) + NUHM (urban); ASHA workers = 1 per 1000 population in rural areas
oneliners.mnemonic: ASHA = Community health link worker at village level

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY): cash incentive for institutional delivery; targets BPL + SC/ST mothers; 600 Rs urban, 1400 Rs rural

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: ICDS (Integrated Child Development Services): nutrition + health + education for children <6 years + pregnant/lactating women via Anganwadi

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Wellcome classification of PEM: <60% expected weight = marasmus; 60-80% with edema = kwashiorkor; 60-80% no edema = undernutrition

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: RDA of iron: adult male = 17 mg/day; adult female = 21 mg/day; pregnancy = 35 mg/day (Indian ICMR recommendations)

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Iodine deficiency disorders (IDD): goiter, cretinism, myxedema; commonest nutritional deficiency in Himalayan belt

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Mid-upper arm circumference (MUAC): <11.5 cm = severe acute malnutrition; 11.5-12.5 cm = moderate SAM in children <5

oneliners.subject: PSM
oneliners.fact: Protein requirement: 0.8 g/kg/day for adults; 1.0-1.5 g/kg/day during pregnancy; 2-3 g/kg/day for premature neonates

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Staphylococcus aureus: MRSA treated with vancomycin; coagulase positive (distinguishes from CoNS); toxic shock syndrome via TSST-1

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Streptococcus pyogenes (Group A Strep): M protein = major virulence factor; causes strep throat leading to rheumatic fever; DOC penicillin
oneliners.mnemonic: M protein = Major Strep virulence factor

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Clostridium tetani: tetanospasmin toxin blocks inhibitory interneurons (glycine, GABA) leading to spastic paralysis; trismus + risus sardonicus

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Clostridium botulinum: flaccid paralysis; blocks ACh release at NMJ; descending paralysis; food-borne/wound/infant types

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Mycobacterium tuberculosis: acid-fast bacillus (Ziehl-Neelsen stain); Lowenstein-Jensen medium; cord factor (trehalose dimycolate)

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Neisseria meningitidis: gram-negative diplococcus; capsule = main virulence; serogroup B = most common; vaccine for A,C,Y,W135

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Salmonella typhi: intracellular pathogen; Widal (O and H antibodies); DOC ceftriaxone; carrier state in gallbladder

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Vibrio cholerae: rice-water stools; El Tor biotype currently predominant; DOC ORS; antibiotics (doxycycline) reduce duration
oneliners.mnemonic: Rice water stool = Vibrio cholerae calling card

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: H. pylori: gram-negative curved rod; CLO test (urease positive); colonizes antrum; risk factor for gastric Ca and MALT lymphoma

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Klebsiella pneumoniae: causes lobar pneumonia in alcoholics + diabetics; mucoid colonies; currant jelly sputum; DOC carbapenem

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Pseudomonas aeruginosa: grape/corn-like sweet smell; produces pyocyanin (blue-green pigment); DOC = anti-pseudomonal beta-lactam + aminoglycoside

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Treponema pallidum: syphilis agent; dark-field microscopy for diagnosis; FTA-ABS most specific serology; DOC = benzathine penicillin
oneliners.mnemonic: FTA-ABS = most specific syphilis serological test

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: HIV: CCR5 co-receptor used by M-tropic strains; CXCR4 by T-tropic; CD4 >500 asymptomatic; <200 = AIDS; DOC HAART
oneliners.mnemonic: CCR5 = Macrophage tropism; CXCR4 = T-cell tropism

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Hepatitis B: HBsAg = surface antigen (first to appear); HBeAg = active replication + infectivity; Anti-HBs = immunity
oneliners.mnemonic: HBsAg first to appear; Anti-HBs = immunity (vaccine)

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Hepatitis C: RNA virus; no vaccine available; DOC = direct-acting antivirals (sofosbuvir + velpatasvir) = 95%+ cure rate
oneliners.mnemonic: HCV = Curable now with direct-acting antivirals

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Rabies: Negri bodies (pathognomonic) in hippocampal neurons; Purkinje cells; 100% fatal once symptomatic; DOC = post-exposure prophylaxis
oneliners.mnemonic: Negri bodies = Neuron inclusions of Rabies

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Herpes simplex: HSV-1 = oral (cold sores); HSV-2 = genital; latency in sensory ganglia; DOC = acyclovir (viral thymidine kinase activates it)
oneliners.mnemonic: Acyclovir needs viral thymidine kinase to activate

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Influenza: segmented RNA virus; antigenic drift (minor mutations) = epidemic; antigenic shift (major reassortment) = pandemic

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: CMV: commonest intrauterine infection (TORCH); periventricular calcifications; owl-eye intranuclear inclusions; DOC ganciclovir
oneliners.mnemonic: Owl eye inclusions = CMV pathognomonic finding

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: EBV (Epstein-Barr): infectious mononucleosis; heterophile antibodies (Paul-Bunnell test); causes Burkitt lymphoma + nasopharyngeal Ca
oneliners.mnemonic: Paul-Bunnell = EBV mono heterophile antibody test

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Dengue: 4 serotypes; Aedes aegypti vector; NS1 antigen earliest marker; IgM after 5 days; secondary infection leads to DHF risk
oneliners.mnemonic: NS1 = earliest; IgM appears at day 5

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Parvovirus B19: erythema infectiosum (fifth disease); slapped cheek appearance; aplastic crisis in sickle cell patients
oneliners.mnemonic: Slapped cheek rash = Parvo B19 fifth disease

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Plasmodium falciparum: malignant tertian malaria; ring forms + banana-shaped gametocytes; cerebral malaria + blackwater fever
oneliners.mnemonic: Banana gametocytes = P. falciparum signature finding

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Leishmania donovani: kala-azar; transmitted by Phlebotomus sandfly; Aldehyde (formal-gel/Napier's) test; DOC miltefosine (India)

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Entamoeba histolytica: flask-shaped ulcers in colon; amoebic liver abscess (chocolate/anchovy sauce pus); DOC metronidazole
oneliners.mnemonic: Anchovy sauce = Amoebic liver abscess pus

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Wuchereria bancrofti: lymphatic filariasis; Culex mosquito; nocturnal periodicity of microfilariae; DOC diethylcarbamazine (DEC)

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Toxoplasma gondii: cats = definitive host; congenital toxoplasmosis = hydrocephalus + chorioretinitis + intracranial calcifications

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Candida albicans: pseudohyphae + blastoconidia; germ tube positive at 37 degrees (distinguishes from other Candida); DOC fluconazole
oneliners.mnemonic: Germ tube = C. albicans fingerprint test

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Cryptococcus neoformans: encapsulated yeast; India ink stain shows capsule halo; meningitis in HIV (CD4<100); DOC amphotericin B + flucytosine

oneliners.subject: Microbiology
oneliners.fact: Aspergillus fumigatus: septate hyphae at 45-degree angle; angio-invasive; DOC = voriconazole for invasive aspergillosis; galactomannan test used

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Km: substrate concentration at which reaction is half-maximal velocity; lower Km = higher affinity; NOT changed by competitive inhibitor
oneliners.mnemonic: Km = Kinetic affinity measure (love for substrate)

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Competitive inhibition: Km increases, Vmax unchanged; overcome by increasing substrate concentration
oneliners.mnemonic: Compete = Can overcome with more substrate (Vmax ok)

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Non-competitive inhibition: Km unchanged, Vmax decreases; cannot overcome by increasing substrate; binds allosteric site

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Hexokinase vs Glucokinase: hexokinase = low Km (high affinity); glucokinase = high Km + sigmoidal kinetics (liver/beta-cells)
oneliners.mnemonic: Gluco = high Km, induced by insulin, in liver

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: LDH isoenzymes: LDH-1 (heart, RBCs) = flipped ratio LDH1>LDH2 in MI; LDH-5 (liver, skeletal muscle) elevated in hepatitis
oneliners.mnemonic: LDH1 flip ratio = heart attack indicator

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: AST:ALT ratio >2:1 in alcoholic liver disease; ALT more liver-specific; isolated ALT rise suggests viral hepatitis
oneliners.mnemonic: AST:ALT >2 = Alcoholic liver disease (de Ritis ratio)

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: G6PD enzyme: pentose phosphate pathway; produces NADPH (maintains reduced glutathione); deficiency leads to oxidative hemolysis

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Creatine kinase (CK-MB): peaks 18-24 hrs in MI; returns to normal by 48-72 hrs; troponin peaks later but stays elevated longer
oneliners.mnemonic: CK-MB = 24hr peak, Troponin stays days longer

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Glycolysis key enzymes: hexokinase (step 1), PFK-1 (rate-limiting, step 3), pyruvate kinase (step 10); regulated by F-2,6-BP

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Cori cycle: lactate from muscle transported to liver for gluconeogenesis; lactate dehydrogenase converts; costs 6 ATP net
oneliners.mnemonic: Cori = liver recycles muscle lactate into glucose

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Ketone body synthesis in liver: acetyl-CoA to acetoacetate to beta-hydroxybutyrate; used by brain in prolonged fasting/DKA
oneliners.mnemonic: Liver makes ketones; brain uses them in starvation

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: HMP shunt (PPP): produces NADPH + ribose-5-phosphate; occurs in cytoplasm; important in RBCs, adrenal cortex, mammary gland

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Urea cycle: occurs in liver (partly mitochondria, partly cytoplasm); key enzyme CPS-I (mitochondria) commits NH3 to cycle
oneliners.mnemonic: CPS-1 = Commits NH3 to urea cycle (first step)

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Phenylketonuria (PKU): phenylalanine hydroxylase deficiency; elevated phenylalanine causes musty/mousy odor; DOC Phe-restricted diet + sapropterin
oneliners.mnemonic: PKU = Musty smelling urine metabolic error

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Galactosemia: galactose-1-phosphate uridyltransferase deficiency; cataracts + liver disease + intellectual disability; DOC galactose-free diet

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Glycogen storage disease type I (von Gierke): glucose-6-phosphatase deficiency; severe hypoglycemia + hepatomegaly + doll face
oneliners.mnemonic: Von Gierke = no G6Pase = glucose trapped in liver

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Maple syrup urine disease: branched-chain keto acid dehydrogenase deficiency (leucine, isoleucine, valine); sweet urine odor; neurological damage

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Homocystinuria: cystathionine beta-synthase deficiency; downward lens dislocation + Marfanoid habitus + thrombosis; DOC pyridoxine (B6)

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Vitamin B1 (thiamine): required for pyruvate dehydrogenase, alpha-KG dehydrogenase, transketolase; deficiency = Wernicke + Beri-beri

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Vitamin B12 deficiency: subacute combined degeneration of spinal cord (posterior + lateral columns); megaloblastic anemia; glossitis
oneliners.mnemonic: B12 = Backbone of spinal cord vitamin

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Niacin (Vit B3) deficiency: pellagra = 3 D's (Dermatitis + Diarrhea + Dementia); 4th D = Death; Casal necklace rash on neck

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Biotin deficiency: raw egg white consumption (avidin binds biotin); dermatitis + alopecia + neurological symptoms
oneliners.mnemonic: Avidin in raw egg whites = biotin thief

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid): required for hydroxylation of proline/lysine in collagen synthesis; deficiency = scurvy (perifollicular hemorrhage)
oneliners.mnemonic: Scurvy = Collagen can't cross-link without Vit C

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Folic acid: required for purine and dTMP synthesis; deficiency leads to megaloblastic anemia; neural tube defects if deficient periconceptionally
oneliners.mnemonic: Folate = Fetal neural tube protector (take before conception)

oneliners.subject: Biochemistry
oneliners.fact: Fat-soluble vitamins: A, D, E, K (ADEK); stored in liver; toxicity possible with excess; deficiency in fat malabsorption

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Rigor mortis: starts 2-6 hrs after death; complete at 6-12 hrs; disappears in 36-48 hrs; begins in jaw and neck (descends)
oneliners.mnemonic: Rigor goes top to bottom (jaw first)

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Hypostasis (livor mortis): gravitational pooling of blood; appears 2-4 hrs; fixed by 8-12 hrs (cannot be shifted after fixation)

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Time of death estimation: corneal clouding in 2-3 hrs; vitreous K+ used for forensic estimation; tache noire in dry conditions

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Drowning: diatom test (Incinerator test) is gold standard for confirming antemortem drowning; fine froth at mouth/nose

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Carbon monoxide poisoning: carboxyhemoglobin causes cherry red color; CO has 250x affinity for Hb vs O2; DOC 100% O2/hyperbaric O2

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Hanging vs strangulation: in hanging, ligature mark is inverted V above thyroid cartilage; in strangulation, it is horizontal

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Hymen is NOT reliable indicator of virginity; medico-legally, intact hymen does NOT confirm virginity
oneliners.mnemonic: Hymen = NOT proof of virginity (legal fact)

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Semen identification: acid phosphatase test for seminal stain; Florence test (choline + iodine = brown rhomboid crystals); DNA typing is best

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Exhumation: disinterment of buried body; requires order from magistrate; decomposition depends on soil, temperature, moisture
oneliners.mnemonic: Exhumation = legal digging up of buried body

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Indian Evidence Act Sec 112: legitimacy of child born during valid marriage presumed; irrebuttable presumption of paternity
oneliners.mnemonic: Sec 112 IEA = Legitimate child presumption in marriage

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Grievous hurt (IPC Sec 320): 8 types including emasculation, permanent loss of sight/hearing, fracture, dislocation

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Arsenic poisoning: garlic odor + Mees lines on nails + rain drop pigmentation on skin; Marsh test for detection
oneliners.mnemonic: Arsenic = garlic odor + rain drops on skin

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Organophosphate poisoning: miosis + bradycardia + excessive secretions; SLUDGE symptoms; DOC atropine + pralidoxime (2-PAM)
oneliners.mnemonic: 2-PAM reactivates AChE if given within 24-48 hours

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Drowning types: dry drowning (laryngospasm, lungs dry) vs wet drowning (lungs waterlogged); secondary drowning = delayed pulmonary edema

oneliners.subject: Forensic
oneliners.fact: Pugilistic (boxer) posture seen in burn victims: heat causes muscle contraction; NOT a sign of ante-mortem injury
oneliners.mnemonic: Pugilistic = Heat artifact, not evidence of fight

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Circle of Willis: ICA supplies anterior and middle cerebral arteries; basilar artery supplies posterior cerebral; communicating arteries connect them
oneliners.mnemonic: ACA + MCA from ICA; PCA from basilar artery

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Internal capsule: posterior limb carries corticospinal + sensory fibers; anterior limb = frontopontine; genu = corticobulbar fibers
oneliners.mnemonic: Posterior limb = motor and sensory highway

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Facial nerve (CN VII): upper face has bilateral cortical representation so upper face is spared in UMN (central) lesion; LMN = all face involved
oneliners.mnemonic: Upper face = bilateral = spared in stroke (UMN)

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Vagus nerve (CN X): dorsal motor nucleus = parasympathetic to thoracoabdominal viscera; nucleus ambiguus = motor (larynx/pharynx)
oneliners.mnemonic: Vagus = Vast range of visceral effects

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Trigeminal nerve (CN V): V1 = ophthalmic (corneal reflex afferent); V2 = maxillary; V3 = mandibular (motor to masseter)

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Hippocampus: part of limbic system; crucial for memory formation; damaged in Alzheimer disease (neurofibrillary tangles + Abeta plaques)
oneliners.mnemonic: Hippo = memory horse of the brain

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Right recurrent laryngeal nerve loops under subclavian artery; LEFT RLN loops under arch of aorta (longer = more vulnerable to Pancoast tumor)
oneliners.mnemonic: Left RLN = longer, more vulnerable to mediastinal disease

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Phrenic nerve (C3,4,5): motor to diaphragm; sensory to central diaphragm, pericardium; runs anterior to lung hilum

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Thoracic duct: drains all lymph except right head/neck/arm/right thorax; begins at cisterna chyli (L1-2); opens into left subclavian-jugular junction
oneliners.mnemonic: Thoracic duct = most of body's lymph drainage

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Coronary arteries: RCA = right atrium, RV, SA node (60%), AV node (80%); LAD = anterior LV, anterior 2/3 IVS, apex

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Horner syndrome: lesion of sympathetic chain (T1 root to superior cervical ganglion to head); ptosis + miosis + anhidrosis

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Erb's point: medial border of SCM where C5+C6 join to form upper trunk of brachial plexus; commonest birth injury site
oneliners.mnemonic: Erb's point = Birth injury target (C5-C6 junction)

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Portal vein: formed by SMV + splenic vein; portal hypertension leads to portosystemic anastomoses (varices, hemorrhoids, caput medusae)

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: McBurney's point: junction of lateral 1/3 and medial 2/3 of line from ASIS to umbilicus; point of maximal tenderness in appendicitis

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Femoral canal boundaries: medial = lacunar ligament; lateral = femoral vein; anterior = inguinal ligament; posterior = pectineal ligament
oneliners.mnemonic: NAVEL: Nerve Artery Vein Empty canal Lymphatics

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Hesselbach's triangle: boundaries = inguinal ligament (inferior), inferior epigastric vessels (lateral), rectus abdominis (medial); site of direct hernia

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Adrenal glands: right is triangular; left is crescent-shaped; right drains directly into IVC; left drains into left renal vein
oneliners.mnemonic: Right adrenal vein goes to IVC; Left goes to renal vein

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Anatomical snuffbox boundaries: laterally = APL + EPB; medially = EPL; floor = scaphoid + radial artery; tenderness = scaphoid fracture

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Carpal tunnel: contents = FDS, FDP, FPL (9 tendons) + median nerve; NOT ulnar nerve; compression causes thenar wasting (CTS)
oneliners.mnemonic: CTS = median nerve in trouble (9 tendons + 1 nerve)

oneliners.subject: Anatomy
oneliners.fact: Sciatic nerve (L4-S3): divides in popliteal fossa into tibial + common peroneal; prone to injury at piriformis (deep gluteal syndrome)

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: GFR approximately 120 mL/min in adults; measured by inulin clearance (gold standard); estimated by creatinine clearance (Cockcroft-Gault)

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Starling forces in glomerulus: favoring filtration = capillary hydrostatic pressure (55 mmHg); opposing = oncotic pressure (30) + Bowman's pressure (15)

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: ADH (vasopressin): released from posterior pituitary in response to increased osmolality or decreased volume; acts on V2 receptors in CD to insert aquaporin-2

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Aldosterone: acts on principal cells of CD; promotes Na+ reabsorption + K+ secretion + H+ secretion; stimulated by angiotensin II + hyperkalemia

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Renal autoregulation: maintains GFR constant despite BP 80-180 mmHg; myogenic reflex + tubuloglomerular feedback (macula densa)
oneliners.mnemonic: Kidney self-regulates its own filtration rate

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Cardiac output = HR x stroke volume; normal = 5 L/min; cardiac index = CO/BSA (normal 2.5-3.5 L/min/m2)
oneliners.mnemonic: CO = HR x SV; normal CO = 5 L/min

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Starling's law of the heart: increased preload (EDV) leads to increased stroke volume (up to a point); failing heart has flattened Starling curve

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: JVP waveforms: a wave = atrial contraction; c wave = tricuspid closure; x descent = atrial relaxation; v wave = venous filling; y descent = tricuspid opening

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Ventricular action potential phases: 0 = rapid depolarization (Na+); 1 = early repol; 2 = plateau (Ca2+); 3 = rapid repol (K+); 4 = resting

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Pulse pressure = systolic - diastolic; normal approximately 40 mmHg; widened in aortic regurgitation + thyrotoxicosis; narrow in cardiac tamponade
oneliners.mnemonic: Wide PP = AR or thyroid; Narrow = tamponade

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: FEV1/FVC: normal >70-80%; obstructive pattern = FEV1/FVC <70% with low FEV1; restrictive = normal ratio with low FVC

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Oxygen-Hb dissociation curve: right shift by increased CO2, temp, 2,3-DPG, H+ (acidosis) leads to increased O2 release to tissues

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Dead space: anatomical approximately 150 mL; physiological = anatomical + alveolar; Bohr equation measures physiological dead space

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Surfactant: produced by type II pneumocytes; decreases surface tension; prevents alveolar collapse; reduces pressure in small alveoli

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction (HPV): hypoxia causes pulmonary vasoconstriction (opposite to systemic response); redirects blood from poorly ventilated areas

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Resting membrane potential: -70 mV in neurons; maintained by Na-K ATPase (3Na out, 2K in) + K+ leak channels
oneliners.mnemonic: Na-K pump = the -70mV guardian (3Na out, 2K in)

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Action potential: all-or-nothing principle; threshold approximately -55 mV; absolute refractory period (inactivated Na channels) prevents summation

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: CSF: produced by choroid plexus (~500 mL/day); absorbed by arachnoid granulations; normal pressure 70-180 mm H2O; protein 15-45 mg/dL

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: Gate control theory of pain: large A-beta fibers close gate; small C-fibers open gate; explains TENS mechanism of pain relief
oneliners.mnemonic: Large fibers = Close pain gate (TENS works this way)

oneliners.subject: Physiology
oneliners.fact: REM sleep: associated with dreaming; loss of muscle tone (pontine atonia); essential for memory consolidation; EEG shows low-amplitude high-frequency waves
oneliners.mnemonic: REM = Remember = memory consolidation during sleep

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Cholesteatoma: keratinizing squamous epithelium in middle ear; erodes bone (ossicles, tegmen, facial canal); dangerous CSOM type
oneliners.mnemonic: Cholesteatoma = Destructive white pearly tumor in ear

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Meniere's disease: endolymphatic hydrops; triad = episodic vertigo + sensorineural HL + tinnitus; DOC low-salt diet + betahistine

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: BPPV: commonest cause of vertigo; posterior semicircular canal (90%); displaced otoconia; Dix-Hallpike test; DOC Epley maneuver

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Acoustic neuroma (vestibular schwannoma): CN VIII tumor; unilateral SNHL + tinnitus; bilateral in NF-2; MRI with contrast for diagnosis

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Rinne's test: AC > BC = positive (normal); BC > AC = negative Rinne = conductive HL; False negative Rinne in dead ear (better ear hears by BC)

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Weber test: lateralizes to affected ear in conductive HL; lateralizes to better (unaffected) ear in sensorineural HL
oneliners.mnemonic: Weber lateralizes to GOOD ear in SNHL

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Otosclerosis: fixation of stapes footplate; conductive HL; Schwartze sign (flamingo pink blush); DOC stapedectomy

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Ludwig's angina: bilateral submandibular + sublingual + submental space infection; airway emergency; DOC airway management + IV penicillin
oneliners.mnemonic: Ludwig = Life-threatening floor of mouth infection

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Quinsy (peritonsillar abscess): DOC incision + drainage + IV antibiotics; interval tonsillectomy after 6 weeks
oneliners.mnemonic: Quinsy = Drained first, tonsils out 6 weeks later

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: CSF rhinorrhea: Beta-2 transferrin is specific marker; halo sign on filter paper; CT cisternography for localization
oneliners.mnemonic: Beta-2 transferrin = CSF in nose diagnostic proof

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Carcinoma nasopharynx: EBV associated; commonest in southern China; arises from fossa of Rosenmuller; DOC chemoradiation

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Laryngomalacia: commonest cause of stridor in neonates; inspiratory stridor; omega-shaped epiglottis; resolves by 18-24 months

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Epistaxis: commonest site = Kiesselbach's plexus (Little's area) on anteroinferior nasal septum; DOC anterior pack or silver nitrate cautery

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Presbycusis: age-related SNHL; high-frequency first affected; 4 kHz notch on audiogram (noise-induced also); gradually progressive
oneliners.mnemonic: 4 kHz notch = Noise or aging-related hearing loss

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Normal IOP: 10-21 mmHg; measured by Goldmann applanation tonometry (gold standard); elevated IOP seen in glaucoma

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Primary open-angle glaucoma (POAG): commonest glaucoma; painless; optic disc cupping (C/D ratio >0.6); DOC prostaglandin analogues (latanoprost)
oneliners.mnemonic: POAG = Painless progressive optic disc cupping

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Acute angle-closure glaucoma: sudden IOP rise; pain + halos + nausea + fixed mid-dilated pupil; DOC IV acetazolamide + pilocarpine
oneliners.mnemonic: Mid-dilated fixed pupil = ACG emergency sign

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Diabetic retinopathy: commonest cause of blindness 20-65 years (developed world); laser photocoagulation for PDR; anti-VEGF for DME
oneliners.mnemonic: Diabetes = Leading cause of working-age blindness

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Trachoma: Chlamydia trachomatis (serovars A-C); commonest infectious cause of blindness worldwide; DOC azithromycin 1g single dose
oneliners.mnemonic: Trachoma = Trichiasis leading to blindness (WHO priority)

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Cataract: commonest cause of blindness worldwide (and India); DOC phacoemulsification + IOL; congenital cataract = rubella (TORCH)
oneliners.mnemonic: Cataract = Number 1 cause of blindness globally

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Retinal detachment: posterior vitreous detachment leads to tear then fluid under retina; risk factors = myopia, trauma; DOC cryopexy/scleral buckle
oneliners.mnemonic: Curtain coming down = retinal detachment symptom

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Marcus Gunn pupil (RAPD): afferent pupillary defect; swinging flashlight test shows affected eye paradoxically dilates; indicates optic nerve lesion
oneliners.mnemonic: RAPD = Relative Afferent Pupillary Defect (optic nerve)

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Argyll Robertson pupil: accommodates but does NOT react to light (prostitute's pupil); caused by neurosyphilis; midbrain lesion
oneliners.mnemonic: AR pupil = Accommodates but doesn't React to light

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Papilledema: bilateral optic disc swelling from raised ICP; no visual acuity loss early; CP angle tumor, pseudotumor cerebri
oneliners.mnemonic: Papilledema = Pressure pushing optic disc forward

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Age-related macular degeneration (AMD): dry (drusen) vs wet (neovascularization); wet AMD DOC = anti-VEGF (ranibizumab/bevacizumab)
oneliners.mnemonic: Anti-VEGF = Wet AMD treatment of choice

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Retinoblastoma: commonest intraocular tumor in children; leukocoria (white pupillary reflex); RB1 gene (chr 13q14); DOC enucleation
oneliners.mnemonic: Leukocoria + child = Retinoblastoma until proven otherwise

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Hordeolum (stye): infection of Zeis/Moll gland (external) or Meibomian gland (internal = chalazion if chronic); DOC warm compresses

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Bitot's spots: conjunctival xerosis with foamy deposits; indicates vitamin A deficiency; DOC high-dose vitamin A supplementation

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Pemphigus vulgaris: acantholysis (intraepidermal split); IgG against desmoglein 1+3; Nikolsky sign positive; DOC steroids + azathioprine
oneliners.mnemonic: PV = Positive Nikolsky, desmoglein IgG target

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Bullous pemphigoid: subepidermal blister (basement membrane zone); IgG against BP180+BP230; Nikolsky sign negative; older age group
oneliners.mnemonic: BP = Below epidermis; Nikolsky Negative (unlike PV)

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Psoriasis: Munro's microabscesses + Auspitz sign (pinpoint bleeding on scale removal) + Koebner phenomenon; DOC mild = topical steroids

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Tinea capitis: Microsporum (Wood's lamp fluoresces green) vs Trichophyton (no fluorescence); DOC griseofulvin or terbinafine (oral)

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Scabies: Sarcoptes scabiei; burrows in interdigital spaces; intense nocturnal itching; DOC permethrin 5% cream (treat all family members)

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Leprosy: Mycobacterium leprae; Fite-Faraco stain; TT = paucibacillary (negative skin smear); LL = multibacillary (anergic)

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: DOC for multibacillary leprosy (MB): rifampicin + clofazimine + dapsone for 12 months (WHO MDT); paucibacillary = 6 months

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Erythema multiforme: target (iris) lesions; triggered by HSV, Mycoplasma; mild form vs Stevens-Johnson syndrome spectrum

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Stevens-Johnson syndrome/TEN: severe mucocutaneous reaction; >30% BSA = TEN; drugs (sulfonamides, carbamazepine, allopurinol) trigger

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Vitiligo: autoimmune destruction of melanocytes; associated with thyroid disease, DM, Addison's; DOC narrow-band UVB phototherapy

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Acne vulgaris: Cutibacterium acnes; DOC mild-moderate = topical retinoids + benzoyl peroxide; severe cystic = oral isotretinoin

oneliners.subject: ENT/Ophth/Derm
oneliners.fact: Melanoma: ABCDE rule (Asymmetry, Border, Color, Diameter >6mm, Evolution); Breslow thickness = most important prognostic factor
oneliners.mnemonic: Breslow thickness = prognosis king for melanoma
